focus: Record Mission Updates 002 and 003; build the dataset-agnostic loader/evaluator harness; run BM25 baselines on SciFact and NFCorpus against published references; migrate Cranfield onto the shared interface.
reason_started: Operator approved Phase 2 via MDE Control Center (stream stream_mrk72yep_804934e1) and delegated Mission Update recording plus M-0002.1 execution.
reason_ended: Both delegated tasks completed: mission updates recorded and M-0002.1 baselines reproduce published reference numbers with Cranfield parity proven on the shared harness.

validators_executed.validator: node --test (53 tests incl. 6 new harness tests)
published-reference comparison embedded in evaluate-dataset output
validators_executed.note: SciFact +3.9% and NFCorpus +0.7% relative vs published BM25 references; both within sanity range, no harness bug indicated.

validators_executed.validator: Cranfield parity check on shared harness
validators_executed.note: Baseline 0.2995 and prf-rerank 0.3260 match Phase 1 values exactly.

reusable_lessons: The original Cranfield source host (ir.dcs.gla.ac.uk) can time out; the GEN-017 retrieval-pool cache doubles as a full query/qrels backup for reconstruction.
BEIR published BM25 numbers reproduce within a few percent using an english-analyzer index and best_fields multi_match over title/text with tie_breaker 0.5 and pytrec_eval-style linear-gain nDCG.
downstream_impacts: M-0002.2 can extend baselines to the remaining tiers by adding registry fetch/load/eval runs with no new harness code for single-corpus datasets; CQADupStack needs sub-forum loader support.
evidence_quality_notes: All acceptance evidence is live-OpenSearch over full test query sets with exact parity checks; the only non-live path (Cranfield query reconstruction from the pool cache) is provenance-recorded inside the artifact set.
loc_note: Roughly 900 lines added across harness modules, scripts, and tests; net growth is new capability (BEIR support), not duplication - the evaluator supersedes per-dataset copies going forward.
